figures.caption: Fig. 4: Illustration of the implemented functionalities: (a) vision agent’s object tracking, and (b) report agent’s asynchronous LLM system call.
Fig. 7: Illustration of system configuration without LLM based reporting agent: (a) object tracking and video encoding, (b) CPU load and memory usage.
Fig. 2: Multi-agent system architecture: functional elements of the proposed object detection framework and implemented agentic components.

Fig. 3: Software components implementing proposed multi-agent architecture in Fig. 2: (a) modules structure, (b) code snippet from "main.py" illustrating system initialization phase with reporting and communication agent subscription to agent specific router events.

Fig. 6: Multi-agent system operation with user interaction through created natural language interface based on the Slack channel and Ollama event report generation. Both notification and control commands are provided through familiar messaging application, while the object detection and tracking events are triggering combined image and text system responses.
Fig. 1: Developed fast prototyping hardware testbed: (a) custom Raspberry Pi camera setup, (b) MIPI CSI camera board with Sony IMX219 color sensor.

Fig. 5: Characteristics of the messaging application: socket mode communication, message events subscription and the bot token scopes defining what functionality communication and control agents can ask for from the chatbot instance.
